key_insight: Using the reverse Fisher divergence makes gradient descent globally convergent for Gaussian-mixture score matching in settings where the standard forward objective can be unstable or initialization-dependent.
engineering_relevance: This gives a more reliable optimization target for training score-based generative models, diffusion-style estimators, and unnormalized density fitters when mixture structure is present.
method: Replace the forward Fisher divergence with the reverse Fisher divergence, taking expectation under the student distribution.
Analyze gradient descent with a new Lyapunov function to prove monotonic progress and convergence.
Handle both a single-Gaussian teacher and a separated Gaussian-mixture teacher under random initialization.
evidence_signal: The paper provides theoretical convergence guarantees: arbitrary initialization for a single-Gaussian teacher and high-probability global convergence under random initialization plus ildeΩ(1)-separation for mixture teachers.
watch_item: The strongest mixture-theoretic guarantee depends on a separation assumption and random initialization, so performance may be less certain when components overlap heavily.
paper_title: Global Convergence of Gradient Descent for Score Matching in Gaussian Mixtures via Reverse Fisher Divergence
